findings.claim: In mouse visual cortex L2/3 and L5, VIP interneurons preferentially target somatostatin (Sst) interneurons; inhibition from VIP onto Sst cells is far larger than onto pyramidal neurons (~7×), establishing the canonical VIP→Sst disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibition mediated by VIP cells onto Sst-expressing cells was much larger than onto the simultaneously recorded pyramids, (average IPSQ Sst 4.6±1.5pC; average IPSQ Pyr 0.6±0.2pC; n=11; INC VIP→Sst =0.42±0.14pC as compared to the much smaller INC VIP→pyramid =0.06±0.02pC and ) and larger in L2/3 tha
findings.effect_size: IPSQ Sst 4.6±1.5 pC vs IPSQ Pyr 0.6±0.2 pC; INC VIP→Sst = 0.42±0.14 pC vs INC VIP→pyramid = 0.06±0.02 pC
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of VIP-Cre-ChR2 cells with paired recordings of Sst vs pyramid postsynaptic targets in mouse V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inhibition mediated by VIP cells onto Sst-expressing cells was much larger than onto the simultaneously recorded pyramids, (average IPSQ Sst 4.6±1.5pC; average IPSQ Pyr 0.6±0.2pC; n=11; INC VIP→Sst =0.42±0.14pC as compared to the much smaller INC VIP→pyramid =0.06±0.02pC
findings.study_system: mouse V1, in vitro slice with optogenetics + paired recordings

findings.claim: In auditory cortex, optogenetic activation of VIP interneurons disinhibits pyramidal cells by mainly silencing SST and PV interneurons; reinforcement-related basal forebrain inputs recruit this VIP-mediated disinhibitory motif during behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP-expressing interneurons in auditory cortex are activated by reinforcement signals and mediate disinhibition of pyramidal cells by inhibiting other interneurons.
findings.evidence: VIP-expressing interneurons in auditory cortex are activated by reinforcement signals and mediate disinhibition of pyramidal cells by inhibiting other interneurons.
findings.study_system: Mouse auditory cortex in vivo, fear-conditioning, optogenetics + paired recordings (VIP-Cre)

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, vM1 long-range inputs preferentially excite VIP interneurons over pyramidal neurons (~2.9× ratio), and VIP optogenetic activation produces IPSCs in SST interneurons that are >4× larger than in nearby pyramids (440.81±6.96 pA vs 111.40±6.11 pA), establishing a vM1→VIP→SST disinhibitory motif during whisking.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast with fast-spiking interneurons, photo-stimulation of VIP interneurons generated IPSCs more than fourfold larger in a SST interneuron than in the simultaneously recorded pyramidal neuron (pyramidal, 111.40 ± 6.11 pA; SST, 440.81 ± 6.96 pA; P < 0.001, Mann-Whitney U test; ).
findings.effect_size: vM1→VIP/Pyr ratio = 2.88 ± 1.12; VIP→SST/Pyr amplitude ratio = 5.06 ± 2.45; charge ratio = 6.44 ± 3.61; whisking firing-rate ratio for VIP = 3.3 ± 1.23.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell paired recordings + ChR2 photo-stimulation of vM1 axons or local VIP cells in S1 barrel cortex slices; in vivo cell-attached during whisking.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: As a population, VIP interneurons provided significantly larger inhibition to SST interneurons than to pyramidal neurons (SST to pyramidal ratio: amplitude = 5.06 ± 2.45, P = 0.01; charge = 6.44 ± 3.61, P = 0.01; Wilcoxon signed-rank test; ).
findings.study_system: mouse S1 barrel cortex; in vitro slice + in vivo head-fixed

findings.claim: Cingulate (Cg) cortex projections to V1 preferentially recruit VIP interneurons in layer 2/3, producing spatially localized disinhibition by inhibition of SOM cells, and a top-down center-surround organization of pyramidal neuron responses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In response to focal Cg axon activation, SOM+ and PV+ neurons inhibit pyramidal neurons over a broad cortical area (with SOM+ neurons as a major source of inhibition at 200 μm), whereas VIP+ neurons selectively enhance the responses at 0 μm through localized inhibition of SOM+ neurons ( , left).
findings.effect_size: Qualitative: stronger Cg-evoked input to VIP than other interneuron types in L2/3; >2× over Pyr (matched motor-to-S1 pattern).
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of Cg axons in V1 with whole-cell recordings from VIP/SOM/PV/Pyr; in vivo two-photon imaging of receptive fields.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The Cg input was stronger in VIP+ than the other neuron types in layer 2/3 ( ), similar to the motor→somatosensory cortex input ( ).
findings.study_system: mouse V1 with cingulate (Cg) inputs; in vitro + in vivo

findings.claim: Nicotinic agonists selectively depolarize VIP/CCK cortical interneurons but not pyramidal cells, PV-fast spiking, or SST interneurons; the response persists in TTX/low-Ca2+ (postsynaptic), is blocked by DHβE/mecamylamine but not the α7-antagonist MLA, and the responsive cells co-express α4/α5/β2 nAChR subunits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotinic receptor agonists had no effect on pyramidal neurons and on most types of interneurons, including parvalbumin-expressing fast spiking interneurons and somatostatin-expressing interneurons, but selectively excited a subpopulation of interneurons coexpressing the neuropeptides vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and cholecystokinin.
findings.effect_size: qualitative cell-type-specific selectivity; pharmacological identity α4β2-like (lacking α7) nAChR.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell recordings + single-cell RT-PCR in rat neocortical slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The responses were blocked by the nicotinic receptor antagonists dihydro-beta-erythroidine and mecamylamine and persisted in the presence of the alpha7 selective nicotinic receptor antagonist methyllycaconitine, suggesting that the involved nicotinic receptors lacked the alpha7 subunit.

findings.claim: Single-cell two-photon optogenetic activation of individual VIP interneurons in mouse V1 in vivo creates spatially narrow, transient "holes" in the SST inhibitory blanket; VIP axons are narrow and target nearby SST cells, providing focal lateral disinhibition of pyramids.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that VIP interneurons have narrow axons and inhibit nearby somatostatin interneurons, which themselves inhibit pyramidal cells. Moreover, via this lateral disinhibition, VIP cells in vivo make local and transient "holes" in the inhibitory blanket extended by SOM cells.
findings.effect_size: qualitative spatial localization (narrow axons; focal disinhibition).
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon optogenetic activation of single VIP cells with simultaneous two-photon Ca2+ imaging.

findings.claim: Higher-order thalamic (POm) inputs to S1 recruit VIP and PV interneurons while suppressing SST interneurons; the resulting VIP-mediated disinhibition is required for NMDAR-dependent LTP of L2/3 pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Paired activation of cortical and higher-order thalamocortical inputs increased vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and parvalbumin (PV) interneuron (IN) activity and decreased somatostatin (SST) IN activity, which together disinhibited the PNs.
findings.effect_size: Qualitative — VIP/PV activity increased, SST decreased; LTP abolished by VIP IN inactivation.
findings.evidence: Thalamocortical slice paired-pathway activation; VIP IN inactivation/blockade; LTP induction protocols.

findings.claim: In barrel cortex in vivo, optogenetic activation of VIP interneurons disinhibits both pyramidal and FS-PV neurons across cortical layers, but a subset of FS neurons (more prevalent in L5/6) are directly inhibited by VIP, indicating layer-dependent VIP→PV vs VIP→SST→PV connectivity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The majority of FS neurons in all layers were disinhibited by VIP neuron activation, but direct inhibition from VIP to FS neurons also existed and was more prevalent for L5/6 FS neurons ( ; ).
findings.effect_size: SST n=89 (14 whole-cell), VIP n=9; layer-dependent disinhibition.
findings.evidence: In vivo silicon-probe extracellular recordings + ChR2 photo-tagging of VIP, SST, PV in awake barrel cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence:
To detect SST (n = 89, including 14 whole-cell recordings) or VIP (n = 9) neurons, we selectively expressed ChR2 in either type by crossing SST-IRES-Cre or VIP-IRES-Cre mice ( ) with a ChR2 reporter line, Ai32 ( ), and exploited light-driven responses to identify these neurons ( 
- A subset of SST and VIP neurons were labeled to verify their recording locations ( and ).
findings.study_system: mouse S1 barrel cortex; in vivo awake

findings.claim: Local pyramidal cells excite VIP and SOM interneurons at similar connection rates but with opposite short-term dynamics — PC→VIP synapses are depressing while PC→SOM are facilitating; rabies tracing shows VIPs and SOMs receive comparable numbers of local presynaptic inputs from non-overlapping subnetworks; electrical coupling is restricted within (not between) VIP/SOM/PV populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PCs formed excitatory synapses onto VIPs and SOMs at similar rates, but synapses onto VIPs were depressing, while those onto SOMs were facilitating ( ).
findings.effect_size: IPSP between VIP/SOM peak ~2.4±0.4 mV (SOM→VIP, n=7) and ~3.2±0.7 mV (VIP→SOM, n=7); rabies inputs: VIPs 1259±409 presynaptic cells/brain, SOMs 2535± (n=4 VIP-cre, 3 SOM-cre brains).
findings.evidence: Paired recordings in V1 and S1; rabies tracing of presynaptic partners; 2-photon mapping with CaMKIIα-C1V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: According to maximal IPSP amplitudes during the 50 Hz AP train ( ), connections between SOMs and VIPs tended to be the largest among all synapses tested, peaking at 2.4 ± 0.4 mV (SOM->VIP, n = 7) and 3.2 ± 0.7 mV (VIP->SOM, n = 7) hyperpolarization.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 and S1; in vitro slice + rabies + 2P optogenetics

findings.claim: In V1, SOM interneurons fire over a wide ACh concentration range (1 μM–10 mM) while VIP and L1 interneurons only depolarize at ≥100 μM ACh and fire at 10 mM; SOM neurons indirectly inhibit VIP/L1 via interneuron-specific connections, suggesting that VIP/L1 cholinergic activation requires SOM disinhibition or higher ACh concentrations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Interestingly, SOM neurons fired robustly to a wide range of ACh concentrations (1 μM – 10 mM), consistent with their low threshold spiking properties ( ) , while VIP and L1 neurons depolarized at 100 μM ACh but fired only at 10 mM ACh ( : Example responses evoked by 100 μM ACh ; ).
findings.effect_size: ACh dose-response: SOM 1 μM–10 mM, VIP 100 μM–10 mM
findings.evidence: Slice whole-cell recordings from SOM/VIP/L1 with bath-applied ACh; optogenetic Arch silencing of SOM in awake mice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Interestingly, SOM neurons fired robustly to a wide range of ACh concentrations (1 μM – 10 mM), consistent with their low threshold spiking properties ( ) , while VIP and L1 neurons depolarized at 100 μM ACh but fired only at 10 mM ACh

findings.claim: Brain-wide rabies tracing reveals VIP+ interneurons in barrel cortex receive proportionally more cortical (especially top-down) input and less local thalamic input than PV or SST interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: More input to vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP+) neurons than to somatostatin-positive (SOM+) or parvalbumin-positive (PV+) neurons came from the contralateral cortex and from higher-order thalamic regions.
findings.evidence: More input to vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP+) neurons than to somatostatin-positive (SOM+) or parvalbumin-positive (PV+) neurons came from the contralateral cortex and from higher-order thalamic regions.
findings.study_system: Mouse barrel cortex (S1), monosynaptic rabies tracing from VIP-, SST-, PV-Cre mice

findings.claim: In mouse PFC, mediodorsal (MD) and ventromedial (VM) thalamic nuclei differentially engage L1 interneurons: VM strongly drives NDNF+ cells in L1a (146 ± 41 pA EPSC vs 6 ± 2 pA at VIP+; ~26× ratio), while MD activates VIP+ cells in L1b approximately equally to NDNF+; VIP+ and NDNF+ cells segregate into L1b vs L1a sublayers and form distinct disinhibitory micro-circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Trains of VM inputs (5 pulses @ 10 Hz with a 473 nm LED) elicited robust EPSCs at VIP− cells in L1a, which were much stronger than those at VIP+ cells in L1b (VIP− = −146 ± 41 pA, VIP+ = −6 ± 2 pA, p = 0.016; n = 7 pairs, 5 mice) ( – ).
findings.effect_size: VM EPSC ratio VIP-/VIP+ = 25.9 (CI 8.4–82.6, p=0.016); MD ratio = 0.94 (n.s.); higher-order thalamic input fraction VIP+ = 81.7 ± 1.2%, NDNF+ = 79.5 ± 2.2%.
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin photo-stimulation of VM/MD axons + paired whole-cell recordings of VIP+ vs VIP- L1 cells in PFC slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This differential thalamic targeting of L1 interneurons was highlighted by VIP− / VIP+ input ratios, which were close to unity for MD, but an order of magnitude higher for VM (MD = 0.94, CI = −0.03–3.4, p = 0.58; VM = 25.9, CI = 8.4–82.6, p = 0.016; MD vs.
findings.study_system: mouse mPFC slice with thalamic optogenetics

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, higher-order thalamic POm activation evokes layer-specific microcircuit responses: deep pyramids receive fast PV-mediated feedforward inhibition, superficial pyramids receive slow inhibition from 5HT3aR+ (including VIP) interneurons, and SST+ neurons do not receive direct POm input and have suppressed activity during POm stimulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Evoked activity in pyramidal neurons from deep layers is fast and synchronized by rapid feedforward inhibition from GABAergic parvalbumin-expressing neurons, and activity in superficial layers is weaker and prolonged, facilitated by slow inhibition from GABAergic neurons expressing the 5HT3a receptor.
findings.effect_size: Qualitative; differential layer-specific thalamic targeting of GABAergic interneuron classes.
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin photo-stimulation of POm + cell-type-targeted whole-cell recordings in S1 slices.

findings.claim: Locomotion-driven activation of VIP interneurons in mouse V1 is mediated by powerful nicotinic cholinergic input: local blockade of nicotinic but not glutamatergic transmission reduces VIP locomotion responses by >2/3; optogenetic VIP activation reproduces the locomotion-induced gain increase, and focal VIP damage blocks it.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The local blockade of nicotinic cholinergic input, but not of glutamatergic input, reduced the response of VIP neurons to locomotion by more than two thirds, and measurements in vitro disclosed powerful nicotinic cholinergic input to VIP neurons.
findings.effect_size: >2/3 reduction in VIP locomotion response with nicotinic block; cross-correlation >0.4 in 20/28 VIP vs 3/77 non-VIP neurons.
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging during locomotion + local pharmacological blockade; optogenetic activation/ablation; in vitro slice nicotinic recordings.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: While 20/28 imaged VIP neurons had a zero-time cross-correlation larger than 0.4, only 3/77 non-VIP neurons had a zero-time cross-correlation larger than 0.4 ( ), indicating that VIP neurons constitute the majority of the neurons responsive to locomotion in the absence of visual

findings.claim: In hippocampal CA1, VIP+ interneurons are heterogeneous: ~48% co-express calretinin (interneuron-targeting) and ~8% co-express CCK (perisomatic basket cells targeting pyramids); optogenetic activation of VIP+ cells produces net disinhibition of CA1 pyramidal cells, and monosynaptic rabies tracing reveals long-range inputs that may convey reward-related signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We focused on VIP+ INs in the CA1 stratum pyramidale , where the majority (53%) of these INs are located and identifiable as IN-targeting based on high level of calretinin (CR) (48%) and low level of cholecystokinin (CCK) co-expression (8%) ( ) ( ; ; ; ).
findings.effect_size: ~53% of VIP+ INs in CA1 stratum pyramidale; CR-co-express 48%, CCK-co-express 8%
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation/silencing in VIP-IRES-Cre mice during behavior; in vivo Ca2+ imaging; monosynaptic rabies; computational modeling.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: where the majority (53%) of these INs are located and identifiable as IN-targeting based on high level of calretinin (CR) (48%) and low level of cholecystokinin (CCK) co-expression (8%)
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1; in vivo + computational

findings.claim: 5-HT3 receptors mediate fast serotonergic excitation of CGE-derived bipolar/VIP interneurons in neocortex, providing one route for serotonergic modulation of disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Neocortical neurons expressing the serotonin 5-HT3 receptor (5-HT3R) were characterized in rat acute slices by using patch-clamp recordings combined with single-cell RT-PCR and histochemical labeling.
findings.evidence: Neocortical neurons expressing the serotonin 5-HT3 receptor (5-HT3R) were characterized in rat acute slices by using patch-clamp recordings combined with single-cell RT-PCR and histochemical labeling.
findings.study_system: Mouse/rat cortex or hippocampus, see paper

findings.claim: Auditory cortex (A1) projections to V1 preferentially target L1 GABAergic interneurons (excluding VIP+ cells) and suppress L2/3 VIP neurons during sound, a circuit motif that sharpens visual responses across modalities — contrary to canonical disinhibitory motifs in which VIPs are activated by long-range cortical inputs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: As shown by response traces of example cells ( ) and summary plots of relative peak response amplitude ( , see Experimental Procedures), L1 inhibitory neurons, except those positive for VIP which only comprise ~10% of the L1 population ( ), received strong excitatory input from A
findings.effect_size: ~10% L1 population is VIP+; VIP cells suppressed during sound at preferred orientation.
findings.evidence: ChR2 photo-stimulation of A1→V1 axons + paired whole-cell recordings in slices; in vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging of PV/SOM/VIP in L2/3.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: L1 inhibitory neurons, except those positive for VIP which only comprise ~10% of the L1 population
findings.study_system: mouse V1; in vitro slice + in vivo two-photon

findings.claim: Layer 1 contains multiple GABAergic interneuron subtypes (canopy/single-bouquet/elongated neurogliaform) that integrate long-range top-down inputs and locally suppress VIP and SST cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By using longitudinal calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups, we show that layer I (LI) interneurons, delineated by co-expression of the 5HT3a serotonin receptor (5HT3aR) and reelin (Re), display spontaneous calcium transients with the highest degree of synchrony among cell types present in the superficial barrel cortex at postnatal day 6 (P6). 5HT3aR Re interneurons are activated by whisker stimulation during this period, and sensory deprivation induces decorrelation of their activity.
findings.evidence: By using longitudinal calcium imaging in unanesthetized mouse pups, we show that layer I (LI) interneurons, delineated by co-expression of the 5HT3a serotonin receptor (5HT3aR) and reelin (Re), display spontaneous calcium transients with the highest degree of synchrony among cell types present in the superficial barrel cortex at postnatal day 6 (P6). 5HT3aR Re interneurons are activated by whisker stimulation during this period, and sensory deprivation induces decorrelation of their activity.
findings.study_system: Mouse/rat cortex or hippocampus, see paper

findings.claim: Brain-wide qBrain quantitative cell-type mapping reveals stereotyped, sex-dimorphic distributions of PV+, SST+ and VIP+ interneurons; long-range feedforward cortical inputs preferentially target PV+ cells while feedback inputs preferentially target VIP+ cells, supporting cell-type-specific circuit modules.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Long-range feedforward inputs strongly target PV+ neurons, while long-range feedback inputs tend to target VIP+ neurons, which in turn inhibit SST+ neurons ( ; ; ).
findings.effect_size: >95% Cre/marker colocalization in adult isocortex; sex-dimorphic SST+/VIP+ cell numbers in 9 brain areas.
findings.evidence: Whole-brain serial two-photon tomography of Cre-driver/H2B-GFP intersectional reporters; sex-specific quantitative comparisons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: This does not seem to be the case in the adult isocortex, where the Cre-driven labeling of the PV+, SST+, and VIP+ cells colocalizes in >95% cells with the expression of the respective reporter proteins ( ; ; ).

findings.claim: 5-HT3A receptor expression demarcates two electrophysiologically distinct subclasses of cortical CGE-derived interneurons: a VIP-expressing bipolar/bitufted cluster (n=22) and an NPY-expressing cluster (n=31) with more complex dendrites; ~73% of VIP-cluster cells co-express VIP mRNA, forming the principal molecular framework for 5HT3aR+ inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The VIP-cluster of 5-HT 3A Interneurons Unsupervised clustering discriminated another group of cells characterized by a high occurrence of VIP mRNA ( n = 16/22, 73%).
findings.effect_size: VIP cluster n=22, NPY cluster n=31; 16/22 (73%) VIP-cluster cells express VIP mRNA
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp + scRT-PCR + GFP transgenic 5HT3a:GFP mice; unsupervised clustering on 53 cells.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Unsupervised clustering discriminated another group of cells characterized by a high occurrence of VIP mRNA ( n = 16/22, 73%).

findings.claim: In mouse somatosensory cortex, PV-, SST-, and 5-HT3aR-expressing neurons together account for nearly 100% of GABAergic interneurons; ~40% of VIP cells co-express calretinin and all VIP neurons express 5-HT3aR; VIP cells have among the highest input resistance and are dendrite-targeting, with rare basket-type morphologies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The conclusion that PV-, SST-, and 5HT3aR-expressing neurons account for nearly if not all GABAergic interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex implies that other interneuron markers such as the neuropeptides VIP, NPY, and cholecystokinin (CCK) and the Ca 2+ -binding proteins cal
findings.effect_size: Three groups account for ~100%; ~40% of VIP cells express CR; VIP cells have highest input resistance.
findings.evidence: Cumulative review of immunohistochemistry, transgenic and electrophysiological studies in mouse cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A significant fraction of VIP neurons (40%) express CR, which, as described earlier, is also expressed in a fraction of SST neurons, as well as in interneurons that do not express VIP or SST

findings.claim: CR+/VIP+ bipolar interneurons receive predominantly depressing excitatory inputs but produce facilitating outputs onto SOM+ Martinotti cells; the group III metabotropic receptor mGluR7 is presynaptically localized at CR+/VIP+→SOM+ synapses, providing glutamatergic regulation of the disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Interestingly, it appears that inputs to CR+/VIP+ (BCR) neurons are mainly depressing (Porter et al., ; Rozov et al., ), whereas all their outputs are facilitating and, vice versa, for CR+/SOM+ (MCR) neurons inputs are often facilitating whereas their outputs show a target cell t
findings.effect_size: Qualitative: opposite STP polarity for inputs vs outputs of VIP cells; mGluR7 presynaptic on VIP→SOM synapses.
findings.evidence: Anatomical and electrophysiological review/synthesis of CR/VIP and CR/SOM interneurons across rodent cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The group III metabotropic receptor mGluR7 expressed by CR+/VIP+ bipolar neurons (Cauli et al., ) is presynaptically localized onto synapses targeting SOM+ interneurons which suggests a glutamatergic control of disinhibition (Dalezios et al., ).

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, VIP+ interneurons constitute approximately 13% of all GABAergic neurons; most are located in L2/3 (~60%); VIPcre/tdTomato mice are highly sensitive and specific for the entire VIP population, validating Cre-driver-based circuit dissection.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the barrel cortex, approximately 13% of all GABAergic neurons are VIP expressing.
findings.effect_size: ~13% of GABAergic neurons in barrel cortex are VIP+; ~60% in L2/3.
findings.evidence: Quantitative tdTomato vs Vip-FISH and α-VIP immunohistochemistry in VIPcre/tdTomato barrel cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Most VIP neurons are found in layer II/III (∼60%), whereas app

findings.claim: In L2/3 of mouse S1, both PV+ and VIP+ interneurons monosynaptically inhibit Martinotti (SST+) cells but with distinct properties: connection probability ~58% PV→MC vs ~35% VIP→MC; mean IPSC amplitude PV 456.55±43.22 pA vs VIP 275.70±46.59 pA; PV→MC inputs depress while VIP→MC inputs facilitate at high frequencies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The connection probability was higher for PV cells (∼58%, 12/21) than for VIP cells (∼35%, 11/31) ( ).
findings.effect_size: Pcon: PV→MC 12/21 (58%), VIP→MC 11/31 (35%); IPSC amplitude PV 456.55±43.22 pA, VIP 275.70±46.59 pA; PV depressing, VIP facilitating at high freq.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic ChR2 activation + paired whole-cell recordings of PV→MC and VIP→MC pairs in L2/3 S1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PV cells evoked multicomponent IPSC with apparently larger amplitudes (PV: 456.55±43.22 pA; VIP: 275.70±46.59 pA; mean±s.e.m.) ( ).

findings.claim: Paired recordings between L2/3 VIP and L4 SST cells in mouse S1 vs V1 reveal that L4 SST cells are non-Martinotti (nMC) in S1 but Martinotti-like (MC) in V1; ~40–45% of SST cells receive VIP input in both areas with similar unitary properties but distinct short-term plasticity (some facilitating, some stable, a fraction of VIP→nMC depressing).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Approximately 40-45% of tested SST cells were inhibited by VIP cells in both cortices.
findings.effect_size: ~40–45% Pcon for VIP→SST in both S1 and V1; differential STP between VIP→nMC and VIP→MC
findings.evidence: Paired whole-cell patch-clamp + morphological reconstructions of L2/3 VIP onto L4 SST cells in S1 and V1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Approximately 40-45% of tested SST cells were inhibited by VIP cells in both cortices.
findings.study_system: mouse S1 and V1 slice paired recordings

findings.claim: PFC sends monosynaptic long-range GABAergic projections to hippocampus that preferentially inhibit VIP+ interneurons, suppressing the local VIP→SST disinhibitory motif and increasing hippocampal feedforward inhibition; this projection is required for object-location encoding and exploratory behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we describe monosynaptic long-range GABAergic projections from PFC to hippocampus. These preferentially inhibit vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons, which are known to disinhibit hippocampal microcircuits.
findings.effect_size: Cell-type-specific PFC long-range inhibitory projection to VIP+ HC interneurons.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of PFC GABAergic terminals in hippocampus + paired in vivo and slice recordings; behavioral assays.

findings.claim: ChAT-expressing VIP interneurons in rodent mPFC are a local cortical source of acetylcholine that directly excites both interneurons in L1–3 and pyramidal neurons in L2/3 and L6 via fast cholinergic synaptic transmission; ~10–20% of postsynaptic targets also receive GABAergic input from the same ChAT-VIP cell, and unlike regular VIP cells, ChAT-VIP cells do not disinhibit pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both interneurons in layers (L)1-3 as well as pyramidal neurons in L2/3 and L6 receive direct inputs from ChAT-VIP neurons mediated by fast cholinergic transmission. A fraction (10-20%) of postsynaptic neurons that received cholinergic input from ChAT-VIP interneurons also received GABAergic input from these neurons.
findings.effect_size: 10–20% of cells receive both ACh and GABA from same ChAT-VIP neuron
findings.evidence: Channelrhodopsin photo-activation of ChAT-Cre x VIP neurons + paired whole-cell recordings in mPFC slices; behavioral attention testing.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A fraction (10-20%) of postsynaptic neurons that received cholinergic input from ChAT-VIP interneurons also received GABAergic input from these neurons.
findings.study_system: rodent mPFC; in vitro slice + behavior

findings.claim: A subset of CA1 hippocampal VIP+ interneurons (VIP-LRPs) project long-range axons to the subiculum, targeting interneurons in CA1 and both interneurons and pyramidal cells in subiculum; VIP-LRPs are interconnected by gap junctions with sparse spike coupling, decrease activity during theta-run epochs and are more active during quiet wakefulness.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we discover a type of VIP+ neuron whose axon innervates CA1 and also projects to the subiculum (VIP-LRPs). VIP-LRPs show specific molecular properties and target interneurons within the CA1 area but both interneurons and pyramidal cells within subiculum.
findings.effect_size: Gap-junction-coupled subnetwork; sparse electrical spike coupling.
findings.evidence: Anatomical and electrophysiological characterization of VIP+ projection neurons in mouse hippocampus + in vivo recordings.

findings.claim: In mouse V1, top-down feedback projections create a second receptive field outside the classical feedforward RF; PV and VIP interneurons (but not SST) respond robustly to inverse stimuli, suggesting VIP recruitment by feedback projections drives the disinhibitory mechanism that creates the feedback RF.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, both PV and VIP neurons responded to inverse stimuli centered on their ffRF ( , ) and showed size-tuning functions to inverse stimuli that peaked well above their response to the largest classical stimuli.
findings.effect_size: VIP and PV cells robustly respond to inverse stimuli; SOM cells do not — consistent with VIP-mediated disinhibition driving feedback RF.
findings.evidence: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging of L2/3 excitatory and Gad2/PV/VIP/SOM interneurons in mouse V1 with optogenetic feedback manipulation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We hypothesize that SOM inhibitory neurons, which, in contrast to PV and VIP neurons, respond poorly to inverse stimuli while robustly responding to large stimuli covering both fbRFs and ffRFs, could mediate the mutual antagonism consistent with their role in surround suppression .

findings.claim: An adult mouse cortex paired-recording dataset of >11,000 connections quantifies cell-type-specific connection probabilities across 11 morphological classes including VIP-like bipolar cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Since the work of Ramón y Cajal in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, neuroscientists have speculated that a complete understanding of neuronal cell types and their connections is key to explaining complex brain functions.
findings.evidence: Since the work of Ramón y Cajal in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, neuroscientists have speculated that a complete understanding of neuronal cell types and their connections is key to explaining complex brain functions.
findings.study_system: Mouse/rat/cat cortex or hippocampus, see paper

findings.claim: Auditory cortex disinhibitory microcircuit underlying associative fear learning: foot-shock-driven cholinergic activation of layer 1 interneurons inhibits L2/3 PV+ interneurons, disinhibiting pyramidal cells; pharmacological/optogenetic block abolishes learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Fear-conditioning-associated disinhibition in auditory cortex is driven by foot-shock-mediated cholinergic activation of layer 1 interneurons, in turn generating inhibition of layer 2/3 parvalbumin-positive interneurons.
findings.effect_size: Block of disinhibition fully abolishes fear learning.
findings.evidence: In vivo recordings + pharmacology + optogenetics in mouse auditory cortex during fear conditioning.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Importantly, pharmacological or optogenetic block of pyramidal neuron disinhibition abolishes fear learning.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory cortex; in vivo fear conditioning

findings.claim: Cortical ChAT+/VIP+ neurons (~33% subset of VIP interneurons) co-transmit acetylcholine and GABA in a target-specific manner: GABA release is robust onto SST and other VIP interneurons, while ACh is released sparsely and selectively onto L1 interneurons and other VIP+/ChAT+ neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, these cells are capable of releasing ACh, with sparse and highly specific targeting of ACh mostly onto layer 1 interneurons and other cortical VIP + /ChAT + neurons.
findings.effect_size: ChAT+ are ~33% of VIP+ INs; GABA release widespread to SST/VIP, ACh release narrow to L1/VIP+ChAT+
findings.evidence: Cre-dependent optogenetic ChR2 activation, paired voltage-clamp recording with selective receptor antagonists (mAChR/nAChR/GABAA) in mouse cortex slice.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We confirmed using both immunohistochemistry and FISH that cortical ChAT + neurons comprise an ~33% subset of VIP + interneurons.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex (multiple regions); ex vivo paired-patch + optogenetics

findings.claim: Disinhibitory VIP→SST motif in V1 is context-dependent: in darkness locomotion activates VIP and inhibits SST (canonical disinhibition), but in the presence of visual stimuli locomotion increases SST responses to large stimuli AND VIP responses to small stimuli — requiring an extension beyond the classical disinhibition model.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our recordings in darkness were consistent with a "disinhibitory" model in which locomotion activates Vip cells, thus inhibiting Sst cells and disinhibiting Pyr cells. However, the disinhibitory model failed when visual stimuli were present: locomotion increased Sst cell responses to large stimuli and Vip cell responses to small stimuli.
findings.effect_size: Visual stimulus reverses canonical VIP-SST anti-correlation; recurrent network model required.
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon Ca2+ imaging of all four cell types (Pyr/SST/VIP/PV) in mouse V1 with locomotion + visual stimuli; recurrent network model.

findings.claim: In mouse V1 layer 2/3, GABAergic subtypes (PV+, SOM+, VIP+) all show broader orientation/spatial-frequency selectivity than excitatory neurons; selectivity preferences vary by subtype establishing differential recruitment in cortical computation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Excitatory neurons had sharp response selectivity for stimulus orientation and spatial frequency, while all GABAergic subtypes had broader selectivity.
findings.evidence: In vivo 2P calcium imaging combined with immunohistochemistry/genetic labels in mouse V1 L2/3.

findings.claim: In mouse dmPFC during a goal-directed sensory discrimination task, VIP+ and PV+ interneurons preferentially signal punishment-related events (with VIP responding strongly to punishment) while SST+ neurons preferentially signal lick-bout/reward onset — providing distinct cell-type-specific task signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Significant PN responses were found in VIP + , PV + , and PYR neurons ( ; p < 10 −10 , signed rank test), but not in SST + neurons (p = 0.61), while RW responses were larger in PV + neurons than all other cell types.
findings.effect_size: Task-modulated fraction: PV 95.3%, SST 96.9%, VIP 81.2%, PYR 77.3%; CC for VIP=0.31±0.01 (n=390), PYR=0.24±0.01 (n=631).
findings.n: VIP n=390, PV n=243, SST n=388, PYR n=631 task-modulated cells
findings.evidence: Cellular-resolution microendoscopic Ca2+ imaging in mouse dmPFC during a sensory discrimination task with rewards and punishments.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: However, the percentage of neurons that were significantly modulated by the task (p < 0.01, 3-way ANOVA, see Experimental Procedures) differed across cell types, higher for PV + (95.3%) and SST + (96.9%) than for VIP + (81.2%) and PYR (77.3%) neurons.
findings.study_system: mouse dmPFC; in vivo microendoscopic GCaMP6f imaging

findings.claim: Lateral entorhinal cortex (LEC) glutamatergic inputs to hippocampal CA1 directly recruit VIP+ and CCK+ interneurons in stratum lacunosum-moleculare; VIP IN activation provides disinhibitory gating of LEC-driven dendritic spikes in CA1 pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Optogenetic silencing of VIP INs led to a decreased incidence of LEC-driven dendritic spikes, suggesting a disinhibitory gating of dendritic activity by local INs.
findings.effect_size: 20% of recorded VIP INs exhibit LEC-driven action potentials; SLM-localized.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic stimulation of LEC fibers + paired patch + computational modeling in mouse hippocampal CA1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Recordings under drug-free conditions showed robust LEC-driven excitation in VIP INs, with 20% of the recorded VIP INs exhibiting LEC-driven APs.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1; ex vivo + in silico

findings.claim: In awake behaving mouse V1, locomotion increases activity of VIP, SST and PV interneurons during visual stimulation, contradicting the simple disinhibition (VIP→SST→Pyr) model; in darkness most VIP and PV neurons remain locomotion-responsive while SST and excitatory neurons are largely non-responsive — context-dependent disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using in vivo two-photon calcium imaging in layers 2/3 and 4 in mouse V1, we reveal that locomotion increases the activity of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST) and parvalbumin (PV)-positive interneurons during visual stimulation, challenging the disinhibition model. In darkness, while most VIP and PV neurons remained locomotion responsive, SST and excitatory neurons were largely non-responsive.
findings.effect_size: Context dependence: SST suppression by VIP only manifest in darkness, not during visual stimulation.
findings.evidence: In vivo 2P GCaMP6f imaging in L2/3 and L4 of V1 in head-fixed running mice; visual stimuli vs darkness conditions.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 L2/3 and L4; awake in vivo 2P

findings.claim: Cortical 5-HT3aR-expressing GABAergic interneurons split into mutually exclusive Reln+ and VIP+ subtypes during early development; type 2 Htr3a+ cells share gene-enrichment with VIP-expressing subtypes (Vip-Chat, Vip-Gpc3, Vip-Mybpc1, Vip-Parm1, Vip-Sncg) while type 3 cells map to NDNF/Reln-expressing subtypes — establishing the molecular branching of CGE-derived interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Htr3a -GFP+ INs in the GM have been divided in largely mutually exclusive subtypes based on the expression of RELN or VIP.
findings.effect_size: Tasic dataset: 48 VIP+ cortical INs, 33 Reln+ cells matched to type 2 vs type 3 transcriptomic clusters.
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq of Htr3a-GFP+ cortical interneurons during development; cross-validation with Zeisel and Tasic adult datasets.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Similarly, in the Tasic et al . data set we extracted 48 cortical INs classified as belonging to Vip -expressing subtypes ( Vip Chat , Vip Gpc3 , Vip Mybpc1 , Vip Parm1 , Vip Sncg ) and 33 cells belonging to Reln -expressing subtypes.

findings.claim: Cortical interneuron compartment-specific connectivity (PV→soma/AIS, SST→dendrites, VIP→other interneurons) is determined by cell-type-specific transcriptional programs encoding synaptic recognition molecules during early postnatal development.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that whether interneurons form synapses on the dendrites, soma, or axon initial segment of pyramidal cells is determined by synaptic molecules that are expressed in a subtype-specific manner.
findings.evidence: Bulk and single-cell RNA-seq of developing cortical interneuron classes + functional perturbations.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; postnatal development; transcriptomic + functional

findings.claim: In hippocampal CA1, VIP+/calretinin+ interneuron-specific type 3 (IS3) cells preferentially innervate oriens-lacunosum moleculare (OLM) interneurons through dendritic synapses with small unitary IPSCs (low Pr) that summate during high-frequency firing; VIP+ basket cells (not IS3) provide asynchronous perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal neurons but do not contact O/A interneurons — establishing distinct VIP subclasses with non-overlapping post-synaptic targeting.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that type 3 IS (IS3) cells that coexpress the vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) and calretinin contact several distinct types of interneurons within the hippocampal CA1 stratum oriens/alveus (O/A), with preferential innervation of oriens-lacunosum moleculare cells (OLMs) through dendritic synapses. In contrast, VIP-positive basket cells provided perisomatic inhibition to CA1 pyramidal neurons with the asynchronous GABA release and were not connected with O/A interneurons.
findings.effect_size: IS3→OLM synapses: small amplitude, low Pr, efficient summation at high frequencies.
findings.evidence: Dual simultaneous paired patch-clamp recordings + targeted optogenetic stimulation in acute mouse hippocampal slices.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Furthermore, unitary IPSCs recorded at IS3-OLM synapses had a small amplitude and low release probability but summated efficiently during high-frequency firing of IS3 interneurons.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus CA1; ex vivo paired-patch + optogenetics

findings.claim: VIP+ interneurons are recruited cortex-wide by behavioral reinforcers (reward and punishment), supporting the disinhibitory motif as a generalized cortical mechanism — not just specific to auditory cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we wondered whether VIP neurons are likewise recruited by reinforcement broadly across the cortex.
findings.evidence: In vivo two-photon imaging of VIP-Cre/GCaMP mice across multiple cortical areas during operant reward/punishment tasks.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex multiple areas; in vivo 2P

findings.claim: In V1 of awake mice, loss of VIP-IN activity alters the behavioral state-dependent modulation of SST interneurons and disrupts state-dependent modulation of pyramidal neuron sensory responses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Loss of VIP-IN activity alters the behavioral state-dependent modulation of somatostatin interneurons
findings.evidence: Optogenetic/chemogenetic VIP-IN inactivation + 2P imaging of SST and pyramidal cells in awake mouse V1.

findings.claim: In mouse V1 during a cross-modal attention task, bi-directional optogenetic manipulation of VIP interneurons does NOT replicate or interact with attentional modulation of pyramidal cells; VIP-SOM disinhibition is therefore orthogonal to attention rather than mediating it.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we found that although activity of VIP cells was strongly modulated by attention, optogenetic manipulation of VIP cells did not replicate the effect of attention on pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: All-optical bidirectional VIP optogenetic perturbation + simultaneous 2P imaging of VIP/SOM/PV/Pyr in mouse V1 during cross-modal attention.

findings.claim: Stimulus-specific enhancement (SSE) of mouse V1 responses by repeated visual exposure during locomotion requires GABA — but NOT VIP peptide release — from VIP interneurons; selective deletion of VIP peptide does not block SSE.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Such stimulus-specific enhancement (SSE) is not seen if locomotion is prevented. The effect of locomotion on cortical responses is mediated by vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) positive interneurons, which can release both the peptide and the inhibitory neurotransmitter GABA.
findings.evidence: Conditional VIP peptide knockout vs GAD knockout in VIP-Cre mice + V1 recordings under locomotion and rest.
findings.study_system: mouse V1; conditional KO + chronic recording

findings.claim: GABAb receptor activation INHIBITS BK channels in L2/3 VIP interneurons, paradoxically increasing their intrinsic excitability — opposite to the typical GABAb-mediated suppression seen in PV cells; SST cells are immune.
findings.claim_source_sentence: GABAb receptors (GABAbRs)... Typically, GABAbRs suppress neuronal activity in the cerebral cortex. Previously, we found that neocortical parvalbumin-expressing cells are strongly inhibited through GABAbRs, whereas somatostatin interneurons are immune to this modulation.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp in vitro slices from mouse primary somatosensory cortex L2/3.
findings.study_system: mouse S1 L2/3; ex vivo patch

findings.claim: Comprehensive review establishing that cortical VIP interneurons can mediate either disinhibitory motifs (canonical VIP→SST→Pyr) or direct inhibition depending on layer, region, and target — synthesis of >50 papers across S1, V1, A1, mPFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we review the basic anatomical features, the circuit properties, and the possible role in the relevant behavioral task of a subclass of GABAergic neurons that express vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP).
findings.evidence: Literature review.

findings.claim: In Fragile X (Fmr1 KO) mice, distractors selectively impair visual discrimination as in human FXS; VIP+ interneurons fail to be modulated by incorrect responses during distractor trials, indicating loss of VIP-mediated cognitive flexibility.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) neurons were significantly modulated by incorrect responses in the poststimulus period during early distractor trials in WT mice
findings.evidence: 2P imaging of VIP cells in V1 + behavioral discrimination task in WT and Fmr1 KO mice.
findings.study_system: Fmr1 KO mouse V1; in vivo 2P + behavior

findings.claim: In marmoset primary motor cortex, VIP is unexpectedly expressed in giant L5 Betz pyramidal neurons (limb/axial representation) — challenging the canonical view that VIP marks only inhibitory interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we demonstrate that VIP is also present in the giant layer 5 pyramidal (Betz) neurons which are characteristic of the limb and axial representations of the marmoset primary motor cortex
findings.evidence: Triple-antibody immunohistochemistry of marmoset primary motor cortex (M1).

findings.claim: In mouse presubiculum, anterior thalamic nucleus inputs onto VIP+ interneurons elicit FACILITATING short-term synaptic plasticity (vs depressing onto pyramidal cells), positioning presubicular VIP cells to mediate temporal integration of head-direction signals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Presubicular VIP expressing interneurons receive facilitating excitation from anterior thalamus.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic ChR2 stimulation of anterior thalamic axons + paired patch in mouse presubicular slices.
findings.study_system: mouse presubiculum; ex vivo opto + patch

findings.claim: VIP+ boutons preferentially innervate dendrites (76% of contacts) and somata (91%) of parvalbumin+ interneurons in rat S1, with two PV target subgroups: cluster-1 (26%, sparse innervation) and cluster-2 (74%, dense innervation) — establishing VIP→PV as a direct anatomical substrate.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The synaptic nature of 91% of somatic and 76% of dendritic contacts was verified by electron microscopy. The target PV + neurons were separated in two significantly different groups by means of cluster analysis. The first group (Cluster 1, 26%)
findings.effect_size: Cluster 1: 26% (sparse), Cluster 2: 74% (dense); 91% somatic and 76% dendritic VIP→PV contacts confirmed by EM.
findings.evidence: Cluster analysis of 272 PV+ neurons reconstructed in 3D + electron microscopy of contacts in rat S1.

findings.claim: Comparative meta-analysis: VIP/calretinin disinhibitory circuits across V1, A1, S1 share core principles — preferential VIP→SST targeting, locomotion-driven recruitment, and reinforcement signals — while area-specific differences include layer distribution and SST→VIP feedback strength.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Common Principles in Functional Organization of VIP/Calretinin Cell-Driven Disinhibitory Circuits Across Cortical Areas.
findings.evidence: Review and quantitative meta-analysis across cortical areas.

findings.claim: A subpopulation of cortical VIP-expressing interneurons (notably Mybpc1/L2-3 subclass) bears highly dynamic dendritic spines — turnover comparable to pyramidal neurons — providing a substrate for experience-dependent rewiring of disinhibitory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A subpopulation of cortical VIP-expressing interneurons with highly dynamic spines.
findings.evidence: Chronic 2P imaging of dendritic spines on VIP-INs over weeks in awake mouse V1.

findings.claim: In mouse sensory cortex, VIP interneurons encode sensory features and action signals during behavior but show no direct reward-signal encoding — refining the role of VIP cells as sensory-motor integrators rather than pure reward relays.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP interneurons in sensory cortex encode sensory and action signals but not direct reward signals.
findings.evidence: Cellular-resolution 2P GCaMP6f imaging of VIP cells in mouse sensory cortex during a discrimination task.

findings.claim: In the anterior cingulate cortex (ACC), VIP interneuron representations of anxiety-related and social stimuli are highly unstable across days — exhibiting representational drift unlike PV/SST cells, suggesting a unique role of VIP cells in flexible context encoding.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Highly unstable heterogeneous representations in VIP interneurons of the anterior cingulate cortex.
findings.evidence: Chronic 2P imaging of VIP, SST, and pyramidal cells in mouse ACC across anxiety/social contexts over weeks.

findings.claim: VIP interneurons in mouse motor cortex undergo spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP) at I→I synapses onto Martinotti and basket cells; these plasticity rules are synapse-type-specific and contribute to disinhibitory circuit reconfiguration during motor learning.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The plasticity of inhibitory interneurons (INs) plays an important role in the organization and maintenance of cortical microcircuits.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp pairing protocols + computational modeling in mouse M1 slice.
findings.study_system: mouse M1; ex vivo patch + model

findings.claim: Short-term plasticity at VIP→Martinotti (MC) and VIP→Basket cell (BC) synapses in mouse motor cortex differ markedly: VIP→MC depresses while VIP→BC depresses with distinct kinetics; outputs from VIP cells are uniformly depressing across targets.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP INs play a key disinhibitory role in cortical circuits by inhibiting other IN types, including Martinotti cells (MCs) and basket cells (BCs).
findings.evidence: Paired patch-clamp recordings in mouse M1 slice across VIP→MC and VIP→BC connections.

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, lemniscal VPM and paralemniscal POm thalamic projections drive feedforward inhibition by directly synapsing on VIP and SST interneurons; VPM input is biased toward L4 SST while POm preferentially recruits L2/3 VIP cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Functional thalamocortical innervation of VIP- and SST-expressing GABAergic interneurons in mouse barrel cortex.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic stimulation of VPM and POm fibers + paired patch on VIP and SST cells in mouse S1 slice.
findings.study_system: mouse S1; ex vivo opto + patch

findings.claim: VIP interneurons mediate locomotion- and arousal-dependent modulation of cortical pyramidal neurons via the canonical VIP→SST disinhibitory motif; chemogenetic VIP silencing disrupts state-dependent population dynamics in V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: a class of GABAergic inhibitory interneurons that express vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing interneurons (VIP), namely VIP interneurons, play a key role in the circuits involved in the modulation of cortical activity by behavioral state
findings.evidence: In vivo 2P + chemogenetic VIP silencing in awake mouse V1.

findings.claim: Inhibitory inputs onto barrel cortex VIP-INs mature presynaptically over postnatal development (P8–P36): release probability and quantal content of inhibitory synapses onto VIP cells increase markedly between P14–P16 and P30–P36, paralleling onset of active whisking.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Presynaptic maturation of inhibitory connections onto vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing GABAergic interneurons
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp recordings + minimal stimulation in three age groups (P8-10, P14-16, P30-36) in mouse barrel cortex slices.
findings.study_system: mouse S1; ex vivo developmental patch

findings.claim: In mouse anterior insular cortex (aIC), VIP+ interneurons receive long-range inputs from frontal/limbic regions; chemogenetic activation of aIC VIP cells suppresses sensory adaptive behaviors and disinhibits pyramidal cells via SST inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP-expressing interneurons in the anterior insular cortex contribute to sensory processing to regulate adaptive behavior
findings.evidence: Whole-brain rabies tracing + 2P imaging + optogenetic modulation in freely moving mice during fear conditioning.
findings.study_system: mouse aIC; rabies + in vivo + opto

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, the rare VIP/ChAT interneuron subclass (~0.5% of cortical neurons) suppresses sensory whisker responses in vivo via combined GABA + ACh release; their activation reduces L2/3 pyramidal responses, contrasting the canonical VIP→SST→Pyr disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) and choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) are sparsely distributed throughout the neocortex, constituting only 0.5% of its neuronal population.
findings.evidence: In vivo 2P imaging + chemogenetic activation of VIP/ChAT cells during whisker stimulation.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: constituting only 0.5% of its neuronal population.

findings.claim: Behavioral strategy shapes engagement of the VIP-SST disinhibitory circuit in mouse V1: visual-comparison strategy strongly engages VIP→SST disinhibition while a statistical timing strategy does not, demonstrating top-down control of disinhibition by task strategy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Behavioral strategy shapes activation of the Vip-Sst disinhibitory circuit in visual cortex.
findings.evidence: Dynamic logistic regression modeling of behavior + simultaneous 2P imaging of VIP and SST cells in mouse V1.
findings.study_system: mouse V1; in vivo 2P + behavioral modeling

findings.claim: Patch-PCR of 155 VIP+ interneurons in mouse S1 L2/3 reveals three transcriptomic subclasses (Sncg+, Mybpc1+, Parm1+) with distinct morphology and electrophysiology — extending the Tasic taxonomy and clarifying which VIP subtypes mediate disinhibition vs IN-targeting.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Sncg, Mybpc1, and Parm1 Classify subpopulations of VIP-expressing interneurons in layers 2/3 of the somatosensory cortex
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp + RT-PCR (Patch-PCR) on 155 VIP+ INs in mouse S1 L2/3.

findings.claim: Long-term potentiation (LTP) at excitatory synapses onto L2/3 VIP interneurons in mouse cortex is induced postsynaptically (NMDAR-dependent) but expressed presynaptically — increased release probability after pairing — establishing a heterosynaptic mechanism for tuning disinhibitory circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Postsynaptic Induction and Presynaptic Expression of Long-Term Potentiation at Excitatory Synapses on Layer 2/3 VIP Interneurons
findings.evidence: Whole-cell pairing protocols + paired-pulse ratio analysis in mouse cortex slices.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex L2/3; ex vivo patch

findings.claim: Calretinin-expressing interneurons in rat barrel cortex include VIP-co-expressing bipolar cells that selectively target other GABAergic cells, supporting an interneuron-targeting role analogous to hippocampal CR cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Calretinin (CR)-positive GABAergic (gamma-aminobutyric acidergic) interneurons have been suggested to target preferentially other GABAergic cells in the neocortex.
findings.evidence: Calretinin (CR)-positive GABAergic (gamma-aminobutyric acidergic) interneurons have been suggested to target preferentially other GABAergic cells in the neocortex.
findings.study_system: Mouse/rat cortex or hippocampus, see paper

findings.claim: In mouse V1, optogenetic activation of VIP+ interneurons sharpens the spatial frequency (SF) tuning of pyramidal cells — increasing their selectivity index — establishing a direct VIP role in stimulus-feature tuning beyond gain modulation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we show that interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP +) regulate the spatial frequency (SF) tuning of pyramidal neurons in mouse visual cortex.
findings.evidence: 2P calcium imaging + optogenetic VIP activation in mouse V1.

findings.claim: Tonic GABAA-mediated currents differentially modulate intrinsic excitability of cortical interneurons: VIP cells exhibit weak tonic inhibition while SST cells exhibit strong tonic inhibition — distinguishing extrasynaptic GABAA receptor functional impact across interneuron subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: GABAAR-mediated tonic inhibition differentially modulates intrinsic excitability of VIP- and SST- expressing interneurons
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp in VIP-tdTomato and SST-tdTomato mouse cortex slices.

findings.claim: In mouse dentate gyrus (DG), VIP+ interneurons exhibit distinct morpho-physiological subclasses (basket-like, dendrite-targeting) and form local connections preferentially onto other GABAergic interneurons — extending the canonical hippocampal disinhibitory motif into DG.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Morpho-physiological properties and connectivity of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in the mouse dentate gyrus
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp + biocytin reconstruction in mouse DG slices.
findings.study_system: mouse DG; ex vivo patch + morphology

findings.claim: Whole-brain rabies tracing in mouse primary motor cortex (MOp) reveals that VIP-INs receive long-range inputs from a distinct distribution including thalamus, sensory cortices, and contralateral M1 — with significant inputs from non-motor regions implicating motor learning roles.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Whole-brain mapping of long-range inputs to the VIP-expressing inhibitory neurons in the primary motor cortex.
findings.evidence: Monosynaptic rabies tracing in mouse MOp + whole-brain registration.

findings.claim: Conditional ErbB4 deletion specifically in VIP interneurons disrupts postnatal maturation of cortical circuits, decreasing pyramidal cell excitability, altering network rhythms, and impairing behavior — establishing VIP-INs as a key controller of circuit maturation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using a conditional ErbB4 deletion, we tested the role of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing interneurons in the postnatal maturation of cortical circuits in vivo.
findings.evidence: Conditional ErbB4 KO in VIP-Cre mice + slice physiology + in vivo recordings + behavior.

findings.claim: Conditional MeCP2 loss restricted to VIP interneurons recapitulates Rett-syndrome-like cortical dysfunction and behavioral deficits — demonstrating that VIP-IN MeCP2 is necessary for normal cortical circuit function and identifying VIP-INs as a key cellular substrate.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent work has suggested that vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing (VIP) interneurons may play a critical role in the proper development and function of cortical circuits.
findings.evidence: Conditional Mecp2 KO in VIP-Cre mice + ephys + behavior in mouse cortex.

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, VIP+ axon boutons target interneurons preferentially in superficial layers (L2/3) but ALSO target excitatory pyramidal neurons in deeper layers (L5/6) — providing direct anatomical evidence for layer-dependent VIP→Pyr inhibition contradicting the strict disinhibition view.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Subcellular Targeting of VIP Boutons in Mouse Barrel Cortex is Layer-Dependent and not Restricted to Interneurons.
findings.evidence: Cre-dependent labelling + EM/light microscopy reconstruction of VIP boutons in mouse S1.

findings.claim: During cortical Up states in mouse barrel cortex, SOM and VIP interneurons play distinct roles: SOM cells are more active and provide most dendritic inhibition while VIP cells fire less but provide phase-locked disinhibition; PV cells contribute strongest somatic inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Distinct Roles of SOM and VIP Interneurons during Cortical Up States.
findings.evidence: In vitro slice recordings of Up states + simultaneous patch-clamp of multiple IN types in mouse S1.
findings.study_system: mouse S1; ex vivo Up states

findings.claim: In mouse hippocampal CA1 during free spatial exploration, VIP-INs robustly increase activity in NEW (novel) but not familiar environments; chemogenetic VIP-IN silencing impairs object-location recognition memory — linking VIP disinhibition to novelty detection and memory.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the CA1 hippocampus, vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons (VIP-INs) play a prominent role in disinhibitory circuit motifs.
findings.evidence: Wireless miniscope imaging + chemogenetic VIP-IN inactivation in freely behaving mice during novel object/place tasks.
findings.study_system: mouse CA1; in vivo wireless + DREADD

findings.claim: In reeler mutant mice (no cortical layers), VIP+ interneurons retain target specificity but show INCREASED callosal (contralateral) input to barrel cortex compared to WT — indicating layer-independent local connectivity but altered long-range projections.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Increased Callosal Connectivity in Reeler Mice Revealed by Brain-Wide Input Mapping of VIP Neurons in Barrel Cortex.
findings.evidence: Monosynaptic rabies tracing of VIP cells in barrel cortex of WT vs reeler mice + whole-brain registration.

findings.claim: In mouse dorsomedial prefrontal cortex during a delayed Go/No-Go task, optogenetic activation of SST or PV interneurons (but NOT VIP) impairs delay-period maintenance and task performance — establishing differential interneuron-type contributions to working memory and the role of disinhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, activating vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-positive interneurons did not alter delay activity or task performance — distinguishing VIP from SST/PV in working-memory maintenance.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of PV/SST/VIP cells + 2P imaging in mouse dmPFC during delayed task.

findings.claim: In mouse V1, VIP+ and SST+ disinhibitory circuits provide CONTEXTUAL modulation of pyramidal cell responses based on stimulus-surround similarity: VIP cells preferentially activate when surround differs from center, releasing pyramidal cells from SST inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: we use optical recordings, manipulations, and computational modeling to show that disinhibitory circuits consisting of vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP)-expressing and somatostatin (SOM)-expressing inhibitory neurons modulate responses in mouse visual cortex depending on similarity between center and surround.
findings.evidence: Two-photon imaging + optogenetic VIP/SOM manipulation + computational model of mouse V1 with contextual stimuli.

findings.claim: In mouse mPFC, VIP+ interneurons increase activity in open arms of elevated plus maze and DISINHIBIT prefrontal responses to hippocampal inputs (carrying anxiety information); chemogenetic VIP silencing reduces avoidance behavior — establishing causal VIP→PFC disinhibition role in defensive behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP interneuron activity increases in the open arms and disinhibits prefrontal responses to hippocampal inputs
findings.evidence: Fiber photometry + chemogenetic silencing of VIP cells + recordings of HC inputs in mouse mPFC during EPM.

conflicts.resolution_status: Reward signals may be region/area-specific (auditory cortex strong, visual cortex weaker); discrepancy may reflect task design and cortical area.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP→SST disinhibitory motif is the dominant connection (Pfeffer 2013, Pi 2013); VIP→Pyr small relative IPSC ratio
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP boutons in deeper barrel cortex layers (L5/6) target excitatory pyramidal neurons (Sohn 2017 cercor/bhx220, Yu 2019)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: VIP boutons target only interneurons (canonical) vs target pyramidal cells
conflicts.resolution_status: Layer-dependent: superficial VIP follows canonical disinhibition; deeper layers exhibit direct VIP→Pyr inhibition.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP cells signal task-related events including punishment but not lick/reward in PFC delay tasks (Pinto 2015)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: In delayed Go/No-Go, VIP optogenetic activation does NOT alter delay activity or task performance — distinguishing VIP from PV/SST in working memory (Kamigaki 2017)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: VIP role in working memory and delay-period maintenance
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP cells are reporters of task events but not active maintainers of delay activity; consistent across studies.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP interneuron activity gates sensory cortical pyramidal-cell gain via SST disinhibition (a 'disinhibitory' model: VIP→SST→Pyr).
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Locomotion in mouse V1 elevates SST activity along with VIP, increasing inhibitory drive onto Pyr cells when visual stimuli are present, contradicting a simple disinhibitory model and indicating context-dependent SST recruitment.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Reconciliation depends on stimulus context and brain state. Disinhibition is one of several VIP-mediated motifs.; confidence: high; additional_dois: ['10.1126/science.1254126', '10.1016/j.neuron.2018.03.037', '10.7554/elife.14985']
conflicts.resolution_status: Both views can hold under different conditions: in darkness or quiet cortex, locomotion or behavior recruits VIP and disinhibits Pyr via SST suppression; with visual drive present, SST cells are co-activated by feedforward sensory input and locomotion increases their gain. A recurrent-network model with state-dependent feedforward weights reproduces both regimes (Pala & Petersen 2018; Pakan et al. 2016 elife).

conflicts.paper_a_claim: VIP cells primarily target SST interneurons, with weak or negligible inhibition of PV cells (Pfeffer 2013; Pi 2013).
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP-immunopositive cortical interneurons can selectively innervate PV-containing interneurons via small symmetric synapses, indicating a direct anatomical VIP→PV pathway (Dávid et al. 2007).
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Functional vs. anatomical evidence: anatomical synapses do not always predict strong functional inhibition.; confidence: moderate; additional_dois: ['10.1111/j.1460-9568.2007.05496.x']
conflicts.resolution_status: Both motifs coexist but with different functional weight: paired recordings in mouse V1 show low VIP→PV connection probability (~0.10) compared to VIP→SST (~0.40), but ultrastructural analysis confirms anatomical VIP→PV synapses. The PV target may be a minor pathway with selective behavioral roles (e.g., gating spiking precision).

conflicts.paper_a_claim: All three major interneuron classes receive considerable long-range cortical input, with VIP cells receiving the largest amount (Wall 2016 monosynaptic rabies).
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Some long-range inputs (e.g., from primary motor cortex, cingulate, retrosplenial) strongly recruit VIP but only weakly recruit SST and PV, suggesting VIP-selective long-range targeting rather than uniform input strength (Lee 2013; Zhang 2014).
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Anatomy aggregates all inputs; functional studies isolate single source areas. No real conflict at the data level.; confidence: high; additional_dois: ['10.1126/science.1254126', '10.1038/s41586-019-1716-z']
conflicts.resolution_status: Both are consistent if interpreted at different granularities: brain-wide rabies tracing shows VIP > SST/PV in total cortical input count, while functional optogenetic assays of specific projections (M1→S1, ACC→V1) reveal selective recruitment of VIP. The selectivity emerges at the level of identified projections rather than overall connectivity.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Atallah et al. 2012 (Neuron) reported that PV interneurons LINEARLY transform cortical responses—primarily multiplicative (divisive/contrast) gain control, with PV activation suppressing pyramidal firing proportionally. Wilson, Runyan, Wang & Sur 2012 (Nature) instead showed division by SST and subtraction by PV (i.e., PV produces ADDITIVE/subtractive inhibition while SST produces divisive). Later studies (Lee et al. 2012; Khan et al. 2018) and theory (Seybold et al.) reconciled the discrepancy in part by noting it depends on PV recruitment level, contrast regime, and feature specificity.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Atallah et al. 2012 (Neuron) reported that PV interneurons LINEARLY transform cortical responses—primarily multiplicative (divisive/contrast) gain control, with PV activation suppressing pyramidal firing proportionally. Wilson, Runyan, Wang & Sur 2012 (Nature) instead showed division by SST and subtraction by PV (i.e., PV produces ADDITIVE/subtractive inhibition while SST produces divisive). Later studies (Lee et al. 2012; Khan et al. 2018) and theory (Seybold et al.) reconciled the discrepancy in part by noting it depends on PV recruitment level, contrast regime, and feature specificity.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: PV gain modulation: division vs subtraction
conflicts.resolution_status: partially resolved – likely depends on optogenetic activation regime and circuit context

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Pi et al. 2013 (Nature) and Pfeffer et al. 2013 (Nat Neurosci) describe a canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibition motif as the dominant action of VIP interneurons. However, Karnani et al. 2016 (Neuron 'Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition') argues VIP disinhibition is spatially restricted and only opens 'holes' rather than producing global disinhibition; some studies (Wall et al., Garcia-Junco-Clemente 2017) suggest VIP can also strongly contact PV in certain laminae, complicating the simple SST-only target view.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Pi et al. 2013 (Nature) and Pfeffer et al. 2013 (Nat Neurosci) describe a canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibition motif as the dominant action of VIP interneurons. However, Karnani et al. 2016 (Neuron 'Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition') argues VIP disinhibition is spatially restricted and only opens 'holes' rather than producing global disinhibition; some studies (Wall et al., Garcia-Junco-Clemente 2017) suggest VIP can also strongly contact PV in certain laminae, complicating the simple SST-only target view.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Disinhibition vs direct PV recruitment as the dominant mode of VIP→pyramidal control
conflicts.resolution_status: active – topographic/contextual dependence, layer-specificity

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Classical view (Wonders & Anderson 2006; Marin & Rubenstein 2001): SST+ interneurons are MGE-derived (Lhx6+/Nkx2-1+), VIP/CR+ are CGE-derived (Sp8/Prox1+). However, Ma et al. 2006 and Xu et al. 2006 identified a population that co-expresses SST and calretinin, and Sousa & Anderson 2009 / Hu et al. and others reported Nkx6-2-derived SST+ interneurons with mixed properties, complicating the strict MGE↔SST and CGE↔VIP/CR mapping.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Classical view (Wonders & Anderson 2006; Marin & Rubenstein 2001): SST+ interneurons are MGE-derived (Lhx6+/Nkx2-1+), VIP/CR+ are CGE-derived (Sp8/Prox1+). However, Ma et al. 2006 and Xu et al. 2006 identified a population that co-expresses SST and calretinin, and Sousa & Anderson 2009 / Hu et al. and others reported Nkx6-2-derived SST+ interneurons with mixed properties, complicating the strict MGE↔SST and CGE↔VIP/CR mapping.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Origin of Reelin+/SST-coexpressing CGE-derived interneurons vs MGE-derived SST
conflicts.resolution_status: refined – sublineage diversity within MGE/CGE

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Fu et al. 2014 (Cell) and Pi et al. 2013 promote a model where locomotion/arousal acts via VIP-driven disinhibition through SST suppression. Reimer/Dipoppa et al. 2018 ('Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific') showed the directionality of behavioral-state modulation differs across PV/SST/VIP and depends on cortical area and stimulus context, complicating a single canonical disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Fu et al. 2014 (Cell) and Pi et al. 2013 promote a model where locomotion/arousal acts via VIP-driven disinhibition through SST suppression. Reimer/Dipoppa et al. 2018 ('Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific') showed the directionality of behavioral-state modulation differs across PV/SST/VIP and depends on cortical area and stimulus context, complicating a single canonical disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Behavioral state modulation: VIP-driven disinhibition vs cell-type-context-dependent inhibition
conflicts.resolution_status: active – area/state-specific

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Ma et al. 2006 and Xu/Callaway distinguished Martinotti vs non-Martinotti SST subtypes based on X94/GIN lines. Patch-seq atlases (Gouwens 2020; Tasic 2018) confirm transcriptomic divisions within SST. However, single-marker studies often pool all SST+ cells. Naka et al. 2019 ('Complementary networks') and Munoz et al. 2017 directly contrast deep-layer non-Martinotti vs upper-layer Martinotti circuits, supporting the functional split.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Ma et al. 2006 and Xu/Callaway distinguished Martinotti vs non-Martinotti SST subtypes based on X94/GIN lines. Patch-seq atlases (Gouwens 2020; Tasic 2018) confirm transcriptomic divisions within SST. However, single-marker studies often pool all SST+ cells. Naka et al. 2019 ('Complementary networks') and Munoz et al. 2017 directly contrast deep-layer non-Martinotti vs upper-layer Martinotti circuits, supporting the functional split.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether non-Martinotti SST cells (X94/L4-targeting) form a separate functional class
conflicts.resolution_status: resolved – multiple subtypes confirmed by morpho-electric-transcriptomic data
